_note: All network indicators are stored DEFANGED ([.] for .). The site refangs in-browser only; feeds ship defanged. This keeps the repo safe for AV, mail gateways, and accidental clicks.

incidents.title: Miasma botnet — AsyncAPI npm packages trojanized via GitHub Actions
incidents.summary: A malicious commit to AsyncAPI's repo abused trusted publishing to push five trojanized npm package versions; on module load they fetch an encrypted loader from IPFS and install the cross-platform Miasma botnet (REST C2, shell execution, systemd persistence). If any listed version was installed on or after Jul 14, treat the host as compromised.
incidents.action: Audit lockfiles for the five versions below; on hits, remove, hunt sync.js / .miasma paths and the systemd user service, and rotate every credential the host could reach.

iocs.context: Stage-2 encrypted sync.js — legit IPFS gateway, block/hunt the CID not the gateway
Runtime lock file — reliable infection marker

incidents.title: ACRStealer — fake Claude Code & AI-tool installer sites
incidents.summary: Fake documentation sites for Claude Code, JetBrains, and NotebookLM — pushed via SEO poisoning and paid search ads — trick developers into running installer commands that sideload ACRStealer through a fake Edge Update DLL. Targets developer machines: browser credentials, wallets, and clipboard-swapped crypto addresses.
incidents.action: Block the listed domains; hunt %LocalAppData%\Microsoft\EdgeUpdate\ for MicrosoftEdgeUpdateCore.dll and rundll32 loading its GetTranslateScript export. Devs: install CLIs only from official docs, never from ad results.

iocs.context: Dead-drop page on legit Telegraph service — block the URL, not the domain
Fake Claude Code docs lure (1 of 88 domains — full list in source)
Install path masquerading as Edge Update

incidents.title: Simba Service — WebDAV phishing delivering PureRAT
incidents.summary: A typosquatted Mexican CURP portal drives victims through a search-ms: WebDAV share to RTLO-spoofed .scr payloads; an Inno Setup loader hollows a .NET stealer into a signed process and drops PureRAT. Abuses CVE-2025-33053 — patched since June 2025, so unpatched hosts are the exposure.
incidents.action: Verify CVE-2025-33053 patching; alert when the WebClient service starts (davclnt.dll reaching remote hosts) and on signed binaries spawning children from WebDAV/UNC paths.

iocs.context: Typosquat of Mexican gov CURP portal — lure page
WebDAV payload host masquerading as OneDrive
WebDAV UNC path pattern opened by the search-ms: lure
Inno Setup loader → in-memory .NET stealer → PureRAT
RTLO filename — renders as …rcs.pdf to hide the .scr extension

incidents.title: Iranian-affiliated actors disrupting internet-exposed PLCs (AA26-097A)
incidents.summary: FBI/CISA/NSA report Iranian-affiliated APT actors using leased infrastructure and legitimate Studio 5000 software to access internet-facing Rockwell/Allen-Bradley PLCs (CompactLogix, Micro850), extract project files, and manipulate HMI/SCADA displays; Dropbear SSH deployed for remote access. Victims across US water, energy, and government saw real operational disruption.
facts: TARGETS: US OT — WWS · ENERGY · GOV
incidents.action: Get PLCs off the internet. Query logs for the IPs below (FBI: vet before blocking); monitor OT ports 44818/2222/102/22/502 at the boundary and hunt unexpected Dropbear SSH on endpoints.

iocs.context: Actor access to exposed PLCs — Mar 2026 only
OT protocol ports abused for inbound C2 — monitor at OT boundary
Unexpected Dropbear SSH deployed on victim endpoints for port-22 access

incidents.title: PhantomEnigma — .gov.br portals spreading Node.js backdoor
incidents.summary: Spoofed Brazilian police emails and 20+ compromised .gov.br portals deliver a trojanized Electron app whose Node.js backdoor beacons every 180 seconds. Mail passed SPF/DKIM/DMARC — domain reputation was the attack surface.
incidents.action: Hunt web/proxy logs for the beacon patterns below; don't whitelist .gov.br senders — validate behavior, not domain reputation.

iocs.context: index.js — Node.js backdoor inside patched Boostnote
Lure / C2 — fake police notary theme
Gen-1 beacon pattern — hunt web/proxy logs
Gen-2 beacon — JSON with COMPUTERNAME/USERNAME every 180s
Dropped payload path from the Inno Setup installer

incidents.title: The Gentlemen RaaS + SystemBC — GPO-deployed multi-OS locker
incidents.summary: A human-operated intrusion where a Gentlemen affiliate disabled Defender via PowerShell, tunneled with SystemBC (RC4 SOCKS5), and pushed the locker domain-wide through GPO scheduled tasks; Windows, Linux, NAS, and ESXi builds observed. The behavioral chain — Defender exclusions, GPO NETLOGON drop, named scheduled tasks — outlasts the 20+ rotating hashes.
incidents.action: Alert on Set-MpPreference -DisableRealtimeMonitoring and Defender ExclusionPath C:\; hunt the scheduled tasks and services below and lockers dropped to NETLOGON or C:\ProgramData\r.exe/g.exe/o.exe.

iocs.context: Gentlemen locker (Windows) — 1 of 20 samples in source
Defender kill — hunt in PowerShell logs

iocs.value: UpdateSystem, UpdateUser, DefS, DefU, UpdateGS, UpdateGU

incidents.title: Lazarus (Famous Chollima) PolinRider — dev-account & registry compromise
incidents.summary: North Korea-linked actors compromised legitimate developer accounts across npm, Go Modules, Packagist, and Chrome extensions to reach developer environments in defense, finance, and crypto. Aimed squarely at builder machines and CI — treat dev credential theft as the objective.
incidents.action: Block the domains/IPs below; hunt nwv3u.exe and builds referencing /scratch/zoo/2026/; enforce hardware-key MFA and provenance checks on package publishing accounts.

incidents.title: FSB Center 16 router config theft via SNMP & Smart Install (AA26-194A)
incidents.summary: Russian FSB Center 16 (Berserk Bear / Static Tundra) scans for SNMP agents with default community strings, then uses SNMP Set-Requests against the Cisco config-copy MIB to TFTP running-configs out; Smart Install (CVE-2018-0171) and the SYNful Knock IOS implant also seen. Weak SNMP config, not a fresh 0-day, is the exposure.
incidents.action: Move to SNMPv3 authPriv and kill v1/v2c; disable Smart Install (no vstack) and patch CVE-2018-0171; block TCP 4786, UDP 69/161/162 at the edge; alert on inbound SNMP Set-Requests hitting the config-copy OID.

iocs.context: CISCO-CONFIG-COPY-MIB — config-copy operation abused to exfil running-config
Config-transfer destination address OID — IDS alert target
Cisco Smart Install — disable and block at edge
Smart Install RCE (CVSS 9.8) — patched Mar 2018, still exploited
TFTP exfil channel for stolen configs
Cisco IOS firmware implant; reboot-persistent, woken by crafted TCP SYN magic packet

incidents.title: BrunoStealer — Discord-webhook exfil infostealer
incidents.summary: A Windows infostealer that fingerprints the host, geolocates it via ip-api.com, and exfiltrates over a hardcoded Discord webhook, executed through rundll32/loaddll64. Discord webhook + ip-api lookup pairing is a cheap, high-signal hunt across proxy logs.
incidents.action: Hunt for outbound discord.com/api/webhooks paired with ip-api.com lookups from non-browser processes; block the sample hash below.

iocs.context: Exfil channel — legit service, alert on non-browser use

incidents.title: Doommageddon — double-extortion ransomware
incidents.summary: Encrypts with asymmetric crypto, appends an extension while keeping the original filename, and runs a victim-shaming leak portal; wipes shadow copies before encrypting. The vssadmin/wmic shadow-deletion pair is the pre-encryption tripwire to alert on.
incidents.action: Alert on shadow-copy deletion (below) as a pre-encryption signal; block the ransom-note write pattern and isolate offline backups.

iocs.context: Ransom note dropped in encrypted directories
